_description: Named risk-profile overrides for config.json, applied by /set-risk-profile. See docs/risk-profiles.md for full rationale and when to use each profile.
_experiment_note: Beyond the four-tier risk ladder, `profiles` also holds symbol/wing/credit experiment cells for the paper-only account-size study (see docs/paper-experiments.md), prefixed small-/medium-/large-/explore-. Each pins one (symbol, wing, min-credit) cell via `symbols` + `wing_widths_by_symbol` + `wing_selection`, and opts into staggering (`stagger_entries`) so its `daily_ic_trade_target` spreads across the session via `min_minutes_between_entries` and the entry window. `late_entry_bias_enabled: false` lets spacing govern timing. Account-size character lives in wing width + symbol (dollar risk/IC), not in throttling sample count -- throughput caps (concurrency 4 / daily 6) hold several time-cohorts at once. Reconstruct a strict small account by filtering the tagged rows in analysis. The ladder is untouched (no `symbols`/`stagger_entries` -> prior behavior).

conservative._note: Default: today's settings. Strict IV/credit floors, wide OTM buffers, latest entry time (12:00 PM). Fewest trades (~1–2/day), highest per-trade safety margin. Best for learning, uncertain markets, or recovery after losses.

moderate._note: Tier 1: Relax IV-rank (0.22) and credit floors (0.12), enter earlier (11:00 AM), tighten stop to 0.93 to offset. ~1 more trade/day vs conservative, thinner per-trade credit margin but paired with tighter stop. Use after 2–4 weeks on conservative if entry rejections are high.

aggressive._note: Tier 2: Tier 1 + accept tighter OTM strikes (delta 0.22, calls 0.30% OTM, puts 0.25% OTM); offset with max_concurrent_ics=3 (25% fewer positions) and stop_trigger_ratio=0.90 (5% tighter than conservative). ~2–3 more trades/week, each one riskier but total exposure capped. Requires active stop management. Use after 2+ weeks on moderate with 60%+ win rate.

very-aggressive._note: Tier 3: Tier 2 + regime gates relaxed (VIX pause at 30, ATR pause at 40); trade through higher-vol/trending conditions. Tightest offsets: max_concurrent_ics=2 (50% fewer), stop_trigger_ratio=0.85 (10% tighter than conservative). Highest activity (~3–5 more trades/week on active weeks), highest per-trade risk. FOR DELIBERATE SHORT EXPERIMENTS ONLY — test high-VIX/ATR tactics for 1 week with explicit drawdown limits. Not a permanent trading mode.

small-xsp._note: Small account, cash-settled XSP, narrow wings (2-3), narrowest-clearing. Dollar risk ~$200-300/IC.

small-iwm._note: Small account, physically-settled IWM (~$230), narrow wings (3-5). Exercises the physical force-close + friction path. Dollar risk ~$300-500/IC.

medium-qqq._note: Medium account, physically-settled QQQ, 5-wide fixed. Dollar risk ~$500/IC.

medium-xsp-wide._note: Wing-risk contrast vs small-xsp: same symbol (XSP), wide wings (5-10), widest-first. Isolates the effect of wing width on the same underlying.

large-spx._note: Large account, cash-settled SPX, wide wings (5-10), widest-first. Dollar risk ~$500-1000/IC.

large-spx-trim._note: Isolation cell (vs large-spx control): ONLY change is trimmed allocation -- max_concurrent_ics 4->2 and daily_ic_trade_target 6->3 (half the exposure). Everything else matches large-spx. Measures the effect of reducing SPX size alone. See docs/paper-experiments.md.

large-spx-directional._note: Isolation cell (vs large-spx control): ONLY change is a tighter directional/vol-expansion guard -- regime_vix1d_ratio_pause_threshold 1.30->1.10, so it pauses when near-term (VIX1D) vol is running hot relative to VIX (a trend-day proxy). Everything else matches large-spx. Measures the directional guard alone.

large-spx-gexstrict._note: Isolation cell (vs large-spx control): ONLY change is a stricter GEX gate -- regime_gex_require_positive requires GEX to be confirmed POSITIVE to enter (baseline only pauses on confirmed-negative, so it also trades on unknown/positive GEX). Everything else matches large-spx. Measures requiring positive gamma alone.

large-spx-holdtoexpiry._note: Isolation cell (vs large-spx control): ONLY change is exit policy -- per_side_stop_management false disables the per-side stop, so the condor is HELD TO SETTLEMENT (closed only by force-close/expiry). Tests whether MEIC's per-side stop adds or destroys value (today's SPX losses were stopped-then-expired-against). Everything else matches large-spx.

large-spx-lateonly._note: Isolation cell (vs large-spx control): ONLY change is entry timing -- entry_window_start 10:00->13:00 (power-hour-only entries: less time to be run over, less gamma exposure). Everything else matches large-spx. Measures the effect of late entry alone.

large-spx-gexmag._note: Isolation cell (vs large-spx control): ONLY change is a magnitude-based GEX gate -- regime_gex_min_flip_distance_pct requires spot to sit at least 0.5% from the gamma-flip strike (deep inside positive gamma, where dealer hedging pins price) rather than just positive-signed. Everything else matches large-spx. Measures requiring STRONG positive gamma alone.

explore-spx-tightcredit._note: Credit-floor sweep (stricter): SPX 5-wide, min_credit 0.20 -- fewer, higher-quality entries. Compare fill rate + expectancy vs large-spx (0.15).

explore-xsp-loosecredit._note: Credit-floor sweep (looser): XSP 2-wide, min_credit 0.10, relaxed IV floor (0.22) -- more entries, thinner credit. Compare vs small-xsp (0.12); watch fee drag on the narrow width.
